key_insights.insight: The pursuit of a goal, even an unattainable one, is what gives life meaning and allows us to improve ourselves.
key_insights.quote: But it's not, because as you pursue that goal, you put yourself together and your life does get better and richer and more abundant.

key_insights.insight: Truthfulness, especially when first practiced, requires acknowledging one's own inadequacies, and while painful, it leads to a meaningful life.
key_insights.quote: And so if you aren't truthful,and that means, unfortunately, especiallyat the beginning, when you start to be truthful, it means deeply coming to terms with yourinadequacies in humility.

key_insights.insight: Humility is key to self-improvement, even small acts of self-discipline such as cleaning your room can have profound effects.
key_insights.quote: If you're aboveorganizing what's actually yours, how in the world are you ever going to organize anything else?

key_insights.insight: It is important to balance time spent in the virtual world with time spent on culture and relationships.
key_insights.quote: you want to hit your projects hard but you have to leave in thatnot with entertainment but with culture because those are not the same thing entertainment is an approximation to culture and you need to leave in that with culture that'sbeauty and drama and art and all of that and then with intimate relationships and friendships andwell it's very difficult to get the balance of all that correct.

key_insights.insight: To help someone, ask questions and listen intently without trying to steer them in a particular direction.
key_insights.quote: And if you ask people questions and you really listen, they will untangle themselves.

key_insights.insight: The best way to help someone is not to give advice but to listen.
key_insights.quote: if you want to help someone the best way to help someone is not to give them advicebut to listen to them

key_insights.insight: When facing adversity, honesty and good relationships can prevent a descent into despair.
key_insights.quote: If you're lucky and something terrible comes your way and you're reasonably honest and your relationships are in good order, maybe you won't end up in hell.

key_insights.insight: Evaluate your position in the workplace, if you are being undervalued, make your case, and if you are not heard then look for another job.
key_insights.quote: Here's what I could do for you if you gave me a 40% raise and the opportunity to progress,but I'd like to see a plan for that. And I've been looking for other opportunities before conducting this discussion, and I have some.

actionable_advice.advice: To build self-awareness, ask yourself what's one thing you're doing wrong that you know you could fix, then meditate on the answer.
actionable_advice.how_to: Sit on your bed at night, be desperate, and honestly ask yourself: 'What's one thing I'm doing wrong that I know I'm doing wrong that I could fix, that I *would* fix?'

actionable_advice.advice: To negotiate a raise, update your resume and apply to other jobs first.
actionable_advice.how_to: Update your CV, address any inadequacies realistically, apply for at least 10 jobs, attend a few interviews, and then present your findings and desired terms to your current boss.

actionable_advice.advice: If you find yourself in a dead-end job, analyze why and what you need to say to your boss.
actionable_advice.how_to: Differentiate and analyze the problem in detail, if you feel undervalued then say something along the lines of, 'I'm bringing more value than I'm being compensated for', and if that discussion is not amenable, find another job.

actionable_advice.advice: In conflict, find out what will satisfy the other person and then do your best to do that.
actionable_advice.how_to: Instead of assuming you know what the other person wants, ask them 'What words should I use?' and work with them on a solution.

actionable_advice.advice: Balance time in the virtual world with activities like writing, exercise, relationships, and connection to culture.
actionable_advice.how_to: Try a routine of Kundalini Yoga or other anxiety reducing exercise, then dedicate time to a craft like writing or painting, then walk with a loved one, and only then connect to the virtual world.

memorable_stories: Peterson describes how he lived in a 'frat house' with up to 20 people, highlighting the value of these social experiences in education beyond academics.
Peterson recounts how a former roommate walked a thousand miles with him when he was ill, highlighting the power of lasting friendships formed in shared living spaces.
Peterson talks about people approaching him and pouring out their tragic stories, saying that even in the revelation of their triumph, you can see the initial depths of their despair.
Peterson shares an analogy about Guy Ritchie's King Arthur movie, where Arthur is overwhelmed by visions of his murderous uncle and unable to pick up a sword.
contrarian_claims: Peterson suggests that the reaction to the pandemic may have caused more harm than the virus itself.
Peterson argues that COVID policies were driven by public opinion polls rather than science.
Peterson questions the scientific credibility of evidence supporting the effectiveness of masks and widespread lockdowns.
Peterson states that aiming to be good and praying for happiness is more worthwhile than chasing happiness directly.

Remote working has its place but we do not understand our embodied environments well enough to duplicate them in a healthy and comprehensive manner in the virtual world, so one should proceed with caution.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Jordan Peterson: How To Become The Person You’ve Always Wanted To Be
